place: Taling Ngam, Ko Samui District, Surat Thani, Thailand
title: Wide-Frontage Five-Islands Pavilion Villa - Taling Ngam
blurb: A minimalist two-bedroom pavilion villa with the widest sea frontage of its estate on Koh Samui's quiet south-west coast — full 180-degree views of the Five Islands and Ang Thong Marine Park, an 11.6-metre infinity pool, and direct beach access for spectacular sunsets.
detail:
Set among a handful of villas on a private headland at Taling Ngam, this escape blends seamlessly with the tropical nature around it, set apart by the widest ocean frontage on the estate — an uninterrupted 180-degree panorama of the famous Five Islands and the Ang Thong National Marine Park beyond. Arranged across two minimalist pavilions, it suits honeymooners and small families alike, with a convertible study giving a flexible third bedroom.

Folding glass walls open the open-plan living, dining and kitchen pavilion completely to the sea breeze and an effortless indoor-outdoor flow, while signature neutral tones keep the focus on the view. Multiple lounge zones — an upper terrace, a sun deck and a shaded relaxation area — surround an 11.6-metre infinity pool that appears to spill toward the islands, with a Thai massage spot on the deck.

Direct beach access leads to a quiet shore for snorkelling and kayaking, with a nearby jetty for sunset moments and the InterContinental resort along the coast for dining. Fully staffed with a villa manager (chef on request), the stay includes a welcome drink, daily breakfast, return airport transfers and beachside restaurant shuttles.
